reasoning: The US collected around $82.2b in customs duties in the previous fiscal year. FY 2025 runs from October 1, 2024 to September 30, 2025. Hitting the $500b minimum threshold would require a greater than 500% increase in customs revenue in a single year. Even if a new administration (inaugurated in Jan 2025) aggressively raised tariffs (e.g., blanket 10-20% and 60% on China), the implementation delay and the fact that a quarter of the fiscal year would have elapsed under previous policies makes it mathematically nearly impossible to reach $500b by the end of FY 2025. Given total US imports of around $3-4 trillion, raising an extra $400b+ in 6-8 months would require sustained effective tariff rates much higher than even the proposed extremes. The market price on Polymarket was 0.00, reflecting the physical impossibility of reaching this range in FY 2025. I am therefore submitting the minimum allowed probability of 2%.
